title: AI / Automation Weekly Digest
takeaways: YouTube updated monetization rules to restrict ad revenue for certain AI-generated, low-quality, and “upsetting” videos — clearer lines for creators using synthetic media.
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang secured deals across Japan’s tech ecosystem; coverage framed it as supply-chain and partnership momentum more than a single product launch.
China’s Moonshot AI released a new Kimi model version, with commentary around geopolitics and open competition in frontier models.
TechCrunch’s Equity podcast asked whether Apple’s lawsuit could affect OpenAI’s hardware plans and IPO timeline — legal overhang worth monitoring.
watchNext: Follow developments in Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI and any hardware-roadmap fallout.

sources.title: YouTube clarifies policies around AI slop and upsetting videos
